title: Standard Operating Procedure: Mouse Stereotaxic Intracranial Injection Surgery
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"98s77","text":"The intent of this Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) is to describe procedures for mouse stereotaxic intracranial injection surgery.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"duh5u","text":"RESPONSIBILITY:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bf7db","text":"Principal investigator (PI), their staff, or any individual performing stereotaxic surgery on rodents or assisting in those procedures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ff9s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Necessary tubing to connect equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfrmk","text":"Stereotaxic surgery frame ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"57ear","text":"Drill with 0.5 mm drill bit, power supply, and foot pedal ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cuvu7","text":"Warming pad, anal probe, and power supply","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cv4f4","text":"Injector ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fg82r","text":"Electric clipper ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aptgd","text":"Non-steroidal analgesic (e.g. carprofen)  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lb0d","text":"Sterile 0.9% saline ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l5ha","text":"Antiseptic solution for skin (e.g., povidone-iodine solution and 70% ethanol) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdksg","text":"Sterile ophthalmic ointment ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f23mm","text":"Sterile surgical instruments","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7illp","text":"         1. Scalpel ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpiet","text":"         2. Blunt Forceps","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fa9vk","text":"         3. Dumont forceps","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"depdp","text":"         4. Fine Scissors","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92t3b","text":"         5. Needle holder","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fmnbi","text":"Sterile swabs and gelfoam spears ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87q2b","text":"Sterile drapes ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7poju","text":"Sterile Hamilton syringe (1700 Hmltn, 10uL) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9eldk","text":"Suture material","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Print the surgery documents and prepare them from Chu Lab SharePoint.
Prepare solutions needed for target injections according Chu Lab Inventory and SOPs from the Chu Lab SharePoint. Place solutions on ice, if applicable.
Autoclave the necessary tools to prior to surgery.
Disinfection surgery station and place down a benchpad.
Setup the necessary equipment including: Stereotaxic frame Injector Warming pad Drill Induction Chamber
Check the isoflurane levels. Levels visible on the front of the vaporizer.
Designate a sterile area on the working surface for the sterile material (instruments, suture material, gauze, etc.).
Clean the Hamilton syringe with sterile water, then 70% ethanol then sterile water again. Note: Make sure it is not clogged and that the plunger can move up and down smoothly.
Note: Perform pre-operative procedures at a safe distance from the surgical environment in order to prevent contamination with hair. Anesthetize the animal in induction chamber.
Open valves to oxygen tank and adjust to a flow of 1 L per 00:01:00 . Adjust the level of vaporizer to 3 percent isoflurane in oxygen and wait for the cessation of movement.
Shave top of the head and remove hair. This is often done over trash can or away from surgical setup. Return animal to induction chamber if need before next step.
Secure animal in the stereotaxic frame. Once nose cone is in place, close valve to chamber and open valve to nose cone and reduce vaporizer to 1.5-2 percent isoflurane in oxygen.
Apply ophthalmic ointment in both eyes to prevent corneal desiccation. Reapply as needed.
Place anal probe underneath body to regulate temperature. Heating system prove setpoint is 36.9 °C .
Using 70% ethanol on a sterile cotton swab in a circular motion, sterilize surgery site from the center of the surgical site to the exterior.
Expose the cranium by making a surgical anterior-posterior incision with a scalpel blade and reflect the skin.
Use scope to locate bregma and lambda.
Position needle directly over the intersection with the coronal and sagittal sutures to locate bregma.
Repeat step 17 at the intersection of the lambdoid and sagittal sutures to locate Lambda.
Adjust the level of the skull until there is less than a 50um D-V difference between Bregma and Lambda, and less than a 50um D-V difference between point +2 and -2 mm M-L to Bregma.
Calculate target coordinates for injection site using leveled bregma coordinates.
Move needle to target site and mark site using a pencil.
Raise needle and, using the hand-held drill, make a single burr-hole in the skull at the injection site. Clean hole of any blood or debris using sterile saline and gelfoam spears.

Draw up necessary volume of solution into the syringe. Lower plunger until a drop of solution is visible at the end of the needle to ensure the solution is at the bottom of the needle and there are no air bubbles.
Remeasure the D-V coordinate at the dura and calculate the target D-V coordinate.
Gradually lower the injection needle gradually to target D-V and wait 00:01:00 .
Check that settings on the injector are correct: Syringe Settings: 1700 Hmltn 10 µL 0.460mm. Adjust volume and speed depending on injection. Check injector is set to infusion.
Press START button on injector to begin injection.
Once injection is done, wait 00:03:00 - 00:05:00 before retracting needle to avoid backflow. For 1 mL of 6-OHDA, wait 00:10:00 .
Gradually withdraw needle 500 µm and wait 00:01:00 . Repeat. Continue to withdraw needle until completely removed from the brain.
Repeat the procedure if more than one injection is required. If performing a bilateral set of injections, check that the needle is not blocked before performing the second injection.
Suture skin with sterile suture (polyamide-nylon, PDS, Vicryl; size: 3-0 or 5-0) and sterilize skin with betadine.
Administer carprofen (5mg/kg, s.c.).
Remove animal from stereotaxic frame and place animal into a cage on a warming pad.

Document surgery on a surgical procedure cage card and return cage to appropriate room in the vivarium. If injecting a biohazard (e.g. AAV),the animal must be housed in the CH1 biohazard room for 72:00:00 with appropriate labeling.
Administer Carprofen (5mg/kg, s.c.) the next day. If mouse was injected with 6-OHDA, supplement with additional food (e.g. yogurt drops) to avoid excessive weight loss.
Continue daily observations for 3 days post-operation and record on the back of the surgical procedure cage card.
Check the needle is straight before beginning and replace if it appears bent.
Secure to needle to stereotaxic frame and adjust out of the way to avoid contact with needle.
Then apply povidone-iodine solution using cotton swabs in a circular motion from the center of the surgical site to the exterior. Repeat this process for a second time.
They will become more prominent over time and hydrogen peroxide solution can be applied to the skull to facilitate identification.
Lower needle until it just touches the surface of the skull.
Record the A-P, M-L, and D-V coordinates of Bregma. Note: For assistance, refer to photo reference on surgery document.
Record the A-P, M-L and D-V coordinates of Lambda.
Observe until animal is awaking and regained righting reflexes.
Extend observation period as necessary to ensure a healthy recovery.
creator: Johnson Agniswamy
